hypothesis: If generative chemistry models are retrained on chemically accurate valency data with Kekulized representations (instead of the original valency data), then the generated molecules will exhibit geometries that more closely match GFN2-xTB-optimized structures (smaller bond length differences Δr, angle differences Δθ, torsion differences Δφ, and lower relaxation energy ΔE_relax) because corrected valency rules and reduced aromatic ambiguity yield more chemically plausible geometries.
